model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Duke holds the #1 overall ranking with elite metrics on both ends of the floor, but the absence of starting point guard Caleb Foster (foot fracture) and center Patrick Ngongba II significantly narrows their advantage against a Houston team with top-5 defensive efficiency. Houston's ability to control tempo and force difficult shots could exploit Duke's diminished backcourt depth, but the Blue Devils' superior overall talent and Cameron Boozer's star power should still be enough to prevail in a tight matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Final Four National Semifinal 1: Duke vs Houston
reasoningStep.summary: Duke is the #1 overall seed with elite metrics but faces significant injury concerns against a defensively elite Houston team.
evidence: Duke ranks #1 in both KenPom and Torvik with a 31-2 record, featuring top-4 offense and #1 defense.
Houston ranks #5 in both KenPom and Torvik with a 28-5 record, featuring top-13 offense and top-4 defense.
Duke is missing starting PG Caleb Foster (foot fracture, out indefinitely) and C Patrick Ngongba II, significantly reducing backcourt depth and interior presence.
Houston appears to be at full strength with no major injury concerns reported.
Duke has managed to win without Foster and Ngongba, including the ACC Tournament, showing resilience but close games.
Houston's elite defensive efficiency (rank 4) can challenge Duke's offense, especially with Duke's diminished guard play.
Duke's overall talent depth and Cameron Boozer's star power provide a slight edge despite the injury concerns.

result.snippet: What Is the Status of Caleb Foster ? Junior guard Caleb Foster has been ruled out for the foreseeable future after suffering a fractured right foot in Duke's regular-season finale against North Carolina on March 7. Foster underwent surgery the following day and is not expected to return until the Final Four at the earliest, if Duke advances that far. Head coach Jon Scheyer provided an update ...
